msens 0.3.4

Build an H3 hex grid from a raster by exploding pixels into child hexes

Source: R/hex.R
hex_grid_from_raster.Rd

A res-7 hexagon (~5.16 km^2) is smaller than a 0.05 degree ocean pixel (~23 km^2 at 40N), so indexing each pixel centroid to a hex would under-sample the grid (leaving gaps). Instead this explodes each non-NA pixel (per mask_layer) into the res-res hexes whose center falls inside the pixel (DuckDB h3_polygon_wkt_to_cells), and each hex inherits that pixel's layer values plus area_km2 (h3_cell_area). Because polyfill uses center containment, adjacent pixels partition the hexes with no overlap. Intended for sources at or coarser than the hex resolution (Bio-Oracle, AquaMaps, NCCOS).

Usage

hex_grid_from_raster(
  r,
  con,
  res = HEX_RES,
  mask_layer = names(r)[1],
  out_tbl = NULL
)

Arguments

r

a terra::SpatRaster in EPSG:4326 (layer names are carried through as the per-hex value columns)

con

a DuckDB connection

res

H3 resolution (default HEX_RES)

mask_layer

name of the layer whose non-NA pixels define coverage (default names(r)[1])

out_tbl

optional DuckDB table to (over)write with hex_id as BIGINT; if NULL, a tibble is returned with hex_id as bit64::integer64

Value

the out_tbl name (invisibly) or a tibble

On this page
